name: OWASP Software Assurance Maturity Model (SAMM) 2.0
description: The OWASP Software Assurance Maturity Model (SAMM) is an open framework to help organizations formulate and implement a strategy for software security that is tailored to the specific risks facing the organization. SAMM is built around 5 business functions (Governance, Design, Implementation, Verification, and Operations), each containing 3 security practices with 2 streams at 3 maturity levels. It provides a measurable way for organizations to analyze and improve their software security posture.

controls.title: Strategy and Metrics - Create and Promote - Level 1
controls.description: Identify objectives and means of measuring effectiveness of the security program. Establish a common understanding of your organization's security posture by interviewing stakeholders to understand threats and risk tolerance.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Strategy and Metrics. Stream: Create and Promote. At maturity level 1, the organization performs a basic assessment of its software security posture, identifies key business drivers for security, and begins defining a high-level security strategy.

controls.title: Strategy and Metrics - Create and Promote - Level 2
controls.description: Establish a unified strategic roadmap for software security within the organization. Develop a 1-3 year strategic security plan with budget and milestones aligned to business priorities.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Strategy and Metrics. Stream: Create and Promote. At maturity level 2, the organization has a published, well-defined security roadmap with clear ownership, budget allocation, and measurable milestones that align with the overall business strategy.

controls.title: Strategy and Metrics - Create and Promote - Level 3
controls.description: Align security efforts with the relevant organizational indicators and asset values. Review the plan annually, monitor milestone implementation through dashboards, and adjust based on lessons learned.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Strategy and Metrics. Stream: Create and Promote. At maturity level 3, the security strategy is continuously refined based on organizational metrics, feedback loops, and evolving business conditions, ensuring ongoing alignment with enterprise objectives.

controls.title: Strategy and Metrics - Measure and Improve - Level 1
controls.description: Define metrics with insight into the effectiveness and efficiency of the Application Security Program. Create basic measurement frameworks for program performance.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Strategy and Metrics. Stream: Measure and Improve. At maturity level 1, the organization defines basic security metrics and begins collecting data to understand the current state of application security across the portfolio.

controls.title: Strategy and Metrics - Measure and Improve - Level 2
controls.description: Establish transparency on your AppSec program's performance. Set targets and KPIs with quantifiable performance indicators.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Strategy and Metrics. Stream: Measure and Improve. At maturity level 2, the organization has established quantifiable KPIs and reporting mechanisms that provide stakeholder visibility into security program performance.

controls.title: Strategy and Metrics - Measure and Improve - Level 3
controls.description: Influence the strategy based on the metrics and organizational needs. Continuously improve your program according to measurement results and evolving business context.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Strategy and Metrics. Stream: Measure and Improve. At maturity level 3, metrics actively drive strategic decisions and program adjustments, creating a data-driven feedback loop for continuous security improvement.

controls.title: Policy and Compliance - Policy and Standards - Level 1
controls.description: Develop a library of policies and standards to govern all aspects of software development in the organization. Identify and document governance and compliance drivers relevant to the organization.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Policy and Compliance. Stream: Policy and Standards. At maturity level 1, the organization identifies applicable regulatory, legal, and organizational requirements and begins documenting foundational security policies.

controls.title: Policy and Compliance - Policy and Standards - Level 2
controls.description: Publish the organization's policies as test scripts or run-books for easy interpretation by development teams. Establish application-specific security and compliance baselines.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Policy and Compliance. Stream: Policy and Standards. At maturity level 2, policies are translated into actionable, testable requirements that development teams can directly apply and verify against.

controls.title: Policy and Compliance - Policy and Standards - Level 3
controls.description: Regularly report on policy and standard compliance, and use that information to guide compliance improvement efforts. Measure adherence to policies, standards, and third-party requirements.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Policy and Compliance. Stream: Policy and Standards. At maturity level 3, the organization has comprehensive compliance measurement and reporting, with policy adherence data actively driving improvement initiatives.

controls.title: Policy and Compliance - Compliance Management - Level 1
controls.description: Identify third-party compliance drivers and requirements and map to existing policies and standards. Create a comprehensive inventory of all compliance requirements with scope triggers.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Policy and Compliance. Stream: Compliance Management. At maturity level 1, the organization creates an inventory of external compliance obligations and maps them to existing internal policies and controls.

controls.title: Policy and Compliance - Compliance Management - Level 2
controls.description: Publish compliance-specific application requirements and test guidance. Develop an application requirements library with test scripts for regulatory compliance.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Policy and Compliance. Stream: Compliance Management. At maturity level 2, compliance requirements are translated into testable application-level requirements with clear verification guidance.

controls.title: Policy and Compliance - Compliance Management - Level 3
controls.description: Track and report on each application's compliance status with third-party obligations. Implement comprehensive measurement and reporting tracking compliance across applications with automated testing.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Policy and Compliance. Stream: Compliance Management. At maturity level 3, compliance tracking is automated and provides per-application visibility into the status of all external compliance obligations.

controls.title: Education and Guidance - Training and Awareness - Level 1
controls.description: Offer staff access to resources around the topics of secure development and deployment. Provide security awareness training for all personnel involved in software development.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Education and Guidance. Stream: Training and Awareness. At maturity level 1, the organization provides basic security awareness resources to development staff, establishing a foundation of security knowledge.

controls.title: Education and Guidance - Training and Awareness - Level 2
controls.description: Educate all personnel in the software lifecycle with technology and role-specific guidance, including security nuances of each language and platform.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Education and Guidance. Stream: Training and Awareness. At maturity level 2, training is tailored to specific roles and technologies, ensuring that each team member receives guidance relevant to their daily responsibilities.

controls.title: Education and Guidance - Training and Awareness - Level 3
controls.description: Develop in-house training programs facilitated by developers across different teams. Create standardized in-house guidance reflecting the organization's secure software development standards.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Education and Guidance. Stream: Training and Awareness. At maturity level 3, the organization has mature, internally developed training programs that are continuously updated and delivered by subject matter experts within the organization.

controls.title: Education and Guidance - Organization and Culture - Level 1
controls.description: Identify a Security Champion within each development team. Establish a security leadership role at the team level.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Education and Guidance. Stream: Organization and Culture. At maturity level 1, the organization designates security champions within development teams to serve as local security advocates and points of contact.

controls.title: Education and Guidance - Organization and Culture - Level 2
controls.description: Develop a secure software center of excellence promoting thought leadership among developers. Create an organizational hub advancing security best practices and expert guidance.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Education and Guidance. Stream: Organization and Culture. At maturity level 2, a centralized security center of excellence provides expert guidance, best practices, and thought leadership across the organization.

controls.title: Education and Guidance - Organization and Culture - Level 3
controls.description: Build a secure software community including all organization people involved in software security. Foster organization-wide collaboration and participation in application security initiatives.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Governance. Practice: Education and Guidance. Stream: Organization and Culture. At maturity level 3, security is embedded in the organizational culture with broad participation, cross-team collaboration, and community-driven security initiatives.

controls.title: Threat Assessment - Application Risk Profile - Level 1
controls.description: Best-effort identification of high-level threats to the organization and individual projects. Conduct basic assessment of application risk to evaluate attack likelihood and impact.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Threat Assessment. Stream: Application Risk Profile. At maturity level 1, the organization performs ad-hoc risk assessments for applications to understand their relative risk posture and prioritize security efforts.

controls.title: Threat Assessment - Application Risk Profile - Level 2
controls.description: Standardization and enterprise-wide analysis of software-related threats within the organization. Centralize risk profile inventory across all organizational applications for stakeholder visibility.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Threat Assessment. Stream: Application Risk Profile. At maturity level 2, a standardized risk profiling methodology is applied across all applications, with centralized tracking providing enterprise-wide visibility.

controls.title: Threat Assessment - Application Risk Profile - Level 3
controls.description: Proactive improvement of threat coverage throughout the organization. Review application risk profiles at scheduled intervals to maintain accuracy and reflect current conditions.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Threat Assessment. Stream: Application Risk Profile. At maturity level 3, risk profiles are periodically reviewed and updated, with findings driving proactive improvements to the organization's overall threat coverage.

controls.title: Threat Assessment - Threat Modeling - Level 1
controls.description: Perform best-effort, risk-based threat modeling using brainstorming and existing diagrams with simple threat checklists.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Threat Assessment. Stream: Threat Modeling. At maturity level 1, threat modeling is performed on an ad-hoc basis using lightweight techniques such as brainstorming sessions and simple threat checklists against existing architecture diagrams.

controls.title: Threat Assessment - Threat Modeling - Level 2
controls.description: Standardize threat modeling training, processes, and tools to scale across the organization.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Threat Assessment. Stream: Threat Modeling. At maturity level 2, threat modeling follows a standardized methodology with defined processes, trained practitioners, and consistent tooling across the organization.

controls.title: Threat Assessment - Threat Modeling - Level 3
controls.description: Continuously optimize and automate threat modeling methodology organization-wide.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Threat Assessment. Stream: Threat Modeling. At maturity level 3, threat modeling is deeply integrated into the development lifecycle with automation support, continuous optimization, and feedback from security testing results.

controls.title: Security Requirements - Software Requirements - Level 1
controls.description: Consider security explicitly during the software requirements process. High-level application security objectives are aligned with functional requirements during initial planning.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Security Requirements. Stream: Software Requirements. At maturity level 1, the organization begins incorporating security considerations into the requirements process, ensuring basic security objectives are identified early.

controls.title: Security Requirements - Software Requirements - Level 2
controls.description: Increase granularity of security requirements derived from business logic and known risks. Make structured requirements available for developer teams throughout development.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Security Requirements. Stream: Software Requirements. At maturity level 2, security requirements are detailed, structured, and consistently available to development teams, derived from business context and identified risks.

controls.title: Security Requirements - Software Requirements - Level 3
controls.description: Mandate security requirements process for all software projects and third-party dependencies. Establish a formal requirements framework that product teams must utilize across all initiatives.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Security Requirements. Stream: Software Requirements. At maturity level 3, a formal, mandatory security requirements framework is applied to all projects and third-party integrations, ensuring comprehensive coverage.

controls.title: Security Requirements - Supplier Security - Level 1
controls.description: Evaluate suppliers based on organizational security requirements. Initial assessment of supplier security capabilities and alignment with organizational standards.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Security Requirements. Stream: Supplier Security. At maturity level 1, the organization performs basic security evaluations of third-party suppliers and their alignment with security requirements.

controls.title: Security Requirements - Supplier Security - Level 2
controls.description: Build security into supplier agreements in order to ensure compliance with organizational requirements. Contractual mechanisms formalize security expectations and accountability measures.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Security Requirements. Stream: Supplier Security. At maturity level 2, security requirements are formally incorporated into supplier contracts and agreements, with defined accountability mechanisms.

controls.title: Security Requirements - Supplier Security - Level 3
controls.description: Ensure proper security coverage for external suppliers by providing clear objectives. Comprehensive security objectives guide external supplier engagement and oversight.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Security Requirements. Stream: Supplier Security. At maturity level 3, suppliers are held to comprehensive, measurable security objectives with active oversight and continuous assessment of their security posture.

controls.title: Secure Architecture - Architecture Design - Level 1
controls.description: Insert consideration of proactive security guidance into the software design process. Teams receive training on fundamental security principles applicable to design work.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Secure Architecture. Stream: Architecture Design. At maturity level 1, basic security design principles are introduced to development teams, establishing awareness of secure architecture concepts.

controls.title: Secure Architecture - Architecture Design - Level 2
controls.description: Direct the software design process toward known secure services and secure-by-default designs. Establish and promote common design patterns and security solutions for organizational adoption.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Secure Architecture. Stream: Architecture Design. At maturity level 2, the organization promotes standardized secure design patterns and reference architectures that teams adopt for consistent security implementation.

controls.title: Secure Architecture - Architecture Design - Level 3
controls.description: Formally control the software design process and validate utilization of secure components. Reference architectures are utilized and continuously evaluated for adoption and appropriateness.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Secure Architecture. Stream: Architecture Design. At maturity level 3, secure architecture patterns are mandatory, formally validated, and continuously evaluated to ensure they remain appropriate and effective.

controls.title: Secure Architecture - Technology Management - Level 1
controls.description: Elicit technologies, frameworks and integrations within the overall solution to identify risk. Identify and assess technology components for security risks.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Secure Architecture. Stream: Technology Management. At maturity level 1, the organization inventories the technologies and frameworks in use and performs basic security risk assessments of technology choices.

controls.title: Secure Architecture - Technology Management - Level 2
controls.description: Standardize technologies and frameworks to be used throughout the different applications. Create organizational standards for approved technologies and development stacks.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Secure Architecture. Stream: Technology Management. At maturity level 2, the organization defines and maintains a list of approved technologies and frameworks, standardizing the technology landscape for security consistency.

controls.title: Secure Architecture - Technology Management - Level 3
controls.description: Impose the use of standard technologies on all software development. Enforce standardization across all software development efforts.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Design. Practice: Secure Architecture. Stream: Technology Management. At maturity level 3, technology standardization is enforced across all projects with formal governance processes for introducing new technologies.

controls.title: Secure Build - Build Process - Level 1
controls.description: Build process is repeatable and consistent. Create a formal definition of the build process so that it becomes consistent and repeatable.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Build. Stream: Build Process. At maturity level 1, the build process is formally defined and documented, ensuring consistency and repeatability across the organization.

controls.title: Secure Build - Build Process - Level 2
controls.description: Build process is optimized and fully integrated into the workflow. Automate your build pipeline and secure the used tooling. Add security checks in the build pipeline.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Build. Stream: Build Process. At maturity level 2, the build pipeline is automated with integrated security checks and the build tooling itself is secured against tampering.

controls.title: Secure Build - Build Process - Level 3
controls.description: Build process helps prevent known defects from entering the production environment. Define mandatory security checks in the build process and ensure that building non-compliant artifacts fails.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Build. Stream: Build Process. At maturity level 3, the build pipeline enforces mandatory security gates that prevent non-compliant artifacts from progressing to production.

controls.title: Secure Build - Software Dependencies - Level 1
controls.description: Create records with Bill of Materials of your applications and opportunistically analyze these. Maintain available information on known security issues in dependencies.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Build. Stream: Software Dependencies. At maturity level 1, the organization creates software bills of materials and begins tracking known vulnerabilities in third-party dependencies.

controls.title: Secure Build - Software Dependencies - Level 2
controls.description: Evaluate used dependencies and ensure timely reaction to situations posing risk to your applications. Achieve full transparency of known security issues in dependencies.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Build. Stream: Software Dependencies. At maturity level 2, dependencies are regularly evaluated with full visibility into known vulnerabilities, and timely remediation processes are in place.

controls.title: Secure Build - Software Dependencies - Level 3
controls.description: Analyze used dependencies for security issues in a comparable way to your own code. Handle security issues in dependencies comparable to those in your own code.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Build. Stream: Software Dependencies. At maturity level 3, third-party dependencies are treated with the same security rigor as first-party code, with comprehensive analysis and equivalent remediation processes.

controls.title: Secure Deployment - Deployment Process - Level 1
controls.description: Deployment processes are fully documented. Formalize the deployment process and secure the used tooling and processes.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Deployment. Stream: Deployment Process. At maturity level 1, the deployment process is formally documented with secured tooling and defined procedures.

controls.title: Secure Deployment - Deployment Process - Level 2
controls.description: Deployment processes include security verification milestones. Automate the deployment process over all stages and introduce sensible security verification tests.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Deployment. Stream: Deployment Process. At maturity level 2, the deployment pipeline is automated with integrated security verification milestones at each stage.

controls.title: Secure Deployment - Deployment Process - Level 3
controls.description: Deployment process is fully automated and incorporates automated verification of all critical milestones. Automatically verify integrity of all deployed software.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Deployment. Stream: Deployment Process. At maturity level 3, the entire deployment process is fully automated with comprehensive integrity verification of all deployed artifacts.

controls.title: Secure Deployment - Secret Management - Level 1
controls.description: Introduce basic protection measures to limit access to your production secrets. Establish defined and limited access to production secrets.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Deployment. Stream: Secret Management. At maturity level 1, basic access controls are established for production secrets, limiting who can access sensitive credentials and keys.

controls.title: Secure Deployment - Secret Management - Level 2
controls.description: Inject secrets dynamically during deployment process from hardened storages and audit all human access to them. Enable detection of potential leakage of production secrets.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Deployment. Stream: Secret Management. At maturity level 2, secrets are dynamically injected from hardened vaults during deployment, with audit trails for all human access and leakage detection capabilities.

controls.title: Secure Deployment - Secret Management - Level 3
controls.description: Improve the lifecycle of application secrets by regularly generating them and ensuring proper use. Minimize possibility and ensure timely detection of production secret abuse.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Secure Deployment. Stream: Secret Management. At maturity level 3, secrets are regularly rotated with automated lifecycle management, and abuse detection provides timely alerts for compromised credentials.

controls.title: Defect Management - Defect Tracking - Level 1
controls.description: All defects are tracked within each project. Introduce structured tracking and knowledgeable decision-making.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Defect Management. Stream: Defect Tracking. At maturity level 1, security defects are systematically tracked within projects with structured processes for triage and resolution decisions.

controls.title: Defect Management - Defect Tracking - Level 2
controls.description: Defect tracking used to influence the deployment process. Leverage defect data to guide deployment decisions.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Defect Management. Stream: Defect Tracking. At maturity level 2, defect data actively influences deployment decisions, with quality gates that prevent release of software with unresolved critical defects.

controls.title: Defect Management - Defect Tracking - Level 3
controls.description: Defect tracking across multiple components is used to help reduce the number of new defects.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Defect Management. Stream: Defect Tracking. At maturity level 3, cross-component defect analysis identifies systemic patterns and root causes, driving proactive measures to prevent recurring defect types.

controls.title: Defect Management - Metrics and Feedback - Level 1
controls.description: Regularly go over previously recorded security defects and derive quick wins from basic metrics.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Defect Management. Stream: Metrics and Feedback. At maturity level 1, the organization periodically reviews security defects and extracts basic metrics to identify quick improvement opportunities.

controls.title: Defect Management - Metrics and Feedback - Level 2
controls.description: Rate all security defects over the whole organization consistently and define SLAs for particular severity classes.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Defect Management. Stream: Metrics and Feedback. At maturity level 2, a consistent defect severity rating system is applied organization-wide with defined SLAs for remediation timeframes by severity class.

controls.title: Defect Management - Metrics and Feedback - Level 3
controls.description: Enforce the predefined SLAs and integrate your defect management system with other relevant tooling. Continuously improve metrics and correlate with broader sources.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Implementation. Practice: Defect Management. Stream: Metrics and Feedback. At maturity level 3, SLAs are enforced with automated tooling integration, and defect metrics are correlated with broader data sources for continuous improvement.

controls.title: Architecture Assessment - Architecture Validation - Level 1
controls.description: Identify application and infrastructure architecture components and review for basic security provisioning.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Architecture Assessment. Stream: Architecture Validation. At maturity level 1, the organization identifies key architectural components and reviews them for basic security control provisioning.

controls.title: Architecture Assessment - Architecture Validation - Level 2
controls.description: Validate the architecture security mechanisms. Review the complete provision of security mechanisms in the architecture.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Architecture Assessment. Stream: Architecture Validation. At maturity level 2, comprehensive validation of all security mechanisms within the architecture is performed against defined security requirements.

controls.title: Architecture Assessment - Architecture Validation - Level 3
controls.description: Review the architecture components' effectiveness. Feed the architecture review results back into enterprise architecture, design principles, patterns, and reference architectures.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Architecture Assessment. Stream: Architecture Validation. At maturity level 3, architecture review findings feed back into enterprise reference architectures and design patterns for continuous improvement.

controls.title: Architecture Assessment - Architecture Mitigation - Level 1
controls.description: Review the architecture to ensure baseline mitigations are in place for typical risks.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Architecture Assessment. Stream: Architecture Mitigation. At maturity level 1, the architecture is reviewed to confirm that baseline security mitigations address common and well-known risk categories.

controls.title: Architecture Assessment - Architecture Mitigation - Level 2
controls.description: Analyze the architecture for known threats. Review the complete provision of security mechanisms in the architecture.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Architecture Assessment. Stream: Architecture Mitigation. At maturity level 2, the architecture is systematically analyzed against known threat models to validate comprehensive mitigation coverage.

controls.title: Architecture Assessment - Architecture Mitigation - Level 3
controls.description: Review the architecture effectiveness and feedback results to improve the security of the architecture. Feed findings back to the Secure Architecture practice to enhance reference architectures.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Architecture Assessment. Stream: Architecture Mitigation. At maturity level 3, mitigation effectiveness is measured and findings are fed back to continuously improve reference architectures and security patterns.

controls.title: Requirements-driven Testing - Control Verification - Level 1
controls.description: Opportunistically find basic vulnerabilities and other security issues. Test for software security controls.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Requirements-driven Testing. Stream: Control Verification. At maturity level 1, basic security testing is performed opportunistically to identify common vulnerabilities and verify security control implementation.

controls.title: Requirements-driven Testing - Control Verification - Level 2
controls.description: Derive test cases from known security requirements. Conduct implementation reviews to identify application-specific risks relative to stated security requirements.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Requirements-driven Testing. Stream: Control Verification. At maturity level 2, security test cases are systematically derived from documented requirements, ensuring comprehensive verification of security controls.

controls.title: Requirements-driven Testing - Control Verification - Level 3
controls.description: Perform regression testing with security unit tests. Sustain application security following bug fixes and during maintenance phases.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Requirements-driven Testing. Stream: Control Verification. At maturity level 3, security regression tests are automated and integrated into the CI/CD pipeline, ensuring security is maintained through ongoing changes.

controls.title: Requirements-driven Testing - Misuse/Abuse Testing - Level 1
controls.description: Opportunistically find basic vulnerabilities and other security issues. Perform security fuzzing testing.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Requirements-driven Testing. Stream: Misuse/Abuse Testing. At maturity level 1, basic fuzzing and abuse testing is performed to discover vulnerabilities through unexpected or malicious input patterns.

controls.title: Requirements-driven Testing - Misuse/Abuse Testing - Level 2
controls.description: Create and test abuse cases and business logic flaw tests. Identify application-specific risks through implementation assessment.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Requirements-driven Testing. Stream: Misuse/Abuse Testing. At maturity level 2, structured abuse cases are developed based on business logic and tested systematically to identify application-specific vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Requirements-driven Testing - Misuse/Abuse Testing - Level 3
controls.description: Denial of service and security stress testing. Maintain security posture during modifications and maintenance.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Requirements-driven Testing. Stream: Misuse/Abuse Testing. At maturity level 3, advanced abuse testing includes denial of service and stress testing, with continuous validation that the security posture is maintained through changes.

controls.title: Security Testing - Scalable Baseline - Level 1
controls.description: Perform security testing (both manual and tool based) to discover security defects. Utilize automated security testing tools.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Security Testing. Stream: Scalable Baseline. At maturity level 1, the organization uses automated security testing tools (SAST, DAST) to establish a baseline of security testing across applications.

controls.title: Security Testing - Scalable Baseline - Level 2
controls.description: Employ application-specific security testing automation and conduct regular manual penetration tests.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Security Testing. Stream: Scalable Baseline. At maturity level 2, security testing is customized per application with tuned tool configurations and regular manual penetration testing complements automated scanning.

controls.title: Security Testing - Scalable Baseline - Level 3
controls.description: Integrate automated security testing into the build and deploy process. Embed security testing into development and deployment workflows.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Security Testing. Stream: Scalable Baseline. At maturity level 3, automated security testing is fully integrated into CI/CD pipelines with quality gates that prevent insecure code from reaching production.

controls.title: Security Testing - Deep Understanding - Level 1
controls.description: Perform manual security testing of high-risk components. Expert review to discover security defects.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Security Testing. Stream: Deep Understanding. At maturity level 1, manual expert security testing is focused on high-risk application components to identify complex vulnerabilities that automated tools may miss.

controls.title: Security Testing - Deep Understanding - Level 2
controls.description: Conduct manual penetration testing. Thorough penetration testing with expert analysis.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Security Testing. Stream: Deep Understanding. At maturity level 2, thorough manual penetration testing by security experts provides deep analysis of application security posture beyond automated capabilities.

controls.title: Security Testing - Deep Understanding - Level 3
controls.description: Integrate security testing into development process. Fully embed expert-level security testing into development workflows.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Verification. Practice: Security Testing. Stream: Deep Understanding. At maturity level 3, expert security testing is fully embedded into development workflows, providing continuous deep security analysis throughout the development lifecycle.

controls.title: Incident Management - Incident Detection - Level 1
controls.description: Use available log data to perform best-effort detection of possible security incidents.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Incident Management. Stream: Incident Detection. At maturity level 1, the organization uses available log data and basic monitoring to perform best-effort detection of potential security incidents.

controls.title: Incident Management - Incident Detection - Level 2
controls.description: Follow an established, well-documented process for incident detection, with emphasis on automated log evaluation.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Incident Management. Stream: Incident Detection. At maturity level 2, a documented incident detection process is in place with automated log analysis and correlation to identify security incidents systematically.

controls.title: Incident Management - Incident Detection - Level 3
controls.description: Use a proactively managed process for detection of incidents.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Incident Management. Stream: Incident Detection. At maturity level 3, incident detection is proactively managed with advanced analytics, threat intelligence feeds, and continuous improvement of detection capabilities.

controls.title: Incident Management - Incident Response - Level 1
controls.description: Identify roles and responsibilities for incident response.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Incident Management. Stream: Incident Response. At maturity level 1, the organization defines roles and responsibilities for incident response, establishing a basic framework for handling security incidents.

controls.title: Incident Management - Incident Response - Level 2
controls.description: Establish a formal incident response process and ensure staff are properly trained in performing their roles.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Incident Management. Stream: Incident Response. At maturity level 2, a formal incident response process is documented and staff are trained, ensuring consistent and effective handling of security incidents.

controls.title: Incident Management - Incident Response - Level 3
controls.description: Employ a dedicated, well-trained incident response team.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Incident Management. Stream: Incident Response. At maturity level 3, a dedicated, well-trained incident response team provides expert-level handling with regular exercises and continuous process improvement.

controls.title: Environment Management - Configuration Hardening - Level 1
controls.description: Best-effort hardening of configurations, based on readily available information.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Environment Management. Stream: Configuration Hardening. At maturity level 1, the organization applies basic configuration hardening based on readily available vendor guidance and industry best practices.

controls.title: Environment Management - Configuration Hardening - Level 2
controls.description: Perform consistent hardening of configurations, following established baselines and guidance.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Environment Management. Stream: Configuration Hardening. At maturity level 2, configuration hardening follows established baselines consistently across all environments with documented standards.

controls.title: Environment Management - Configuration Hardening - Level 3
controls.description: Actively monitor configurations for non-conformance to baselines, and handle detected occurrences as security defects.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Environment Management. Stream: Configuration Hardening. At maturity level 3, configuration compliance is actively monitored with automated detection of deviations, which are treated as security defects requiring remediation.

controls.title: Environment Management - Patching and Updating - Level 1
controls.description: Perform best-effort patching of system and application components.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Environment Management. Stream: Patching and Updating. At maturity level 1, the organization performs best-effort patching of systems and applications based on available information about vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Environment Management - Patching and Updating - Level 2
controls.description: Perform regular patching of system and application components, across the full stack. Ensure timely delivery of patches to customers.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Environment Management. Stream: Patching and Updating. At maturity level 2, a regular, comprehensive patching process covers the full technology stack with defined schedules and timely delivery of security updates.

controls.title: Environment Management - Patching and Updating - Level 3
controls.description: Actively monitor update status and manage missing patches as security defects. Proactively obtain vulnerability and update information for components.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Environment Management. Stream: Patching and Updating. At maturity level 3, patch status is actively monitored with missing patches treated as security defects, and proactive vulnerability intelligence drives the patching process.

controls.title: Operational Management - Data Protection - Level 1
controls.description: Implement basic data protection practices. Establish initial safeguards for sensitive information.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Operational Management. Stream: Data Protection. At maturity level 1, the organization implements basic data protection practices and establishes initial safeguards for sensitive information.

controls.title: Operational Management - Data Protection - Level 2
controls.description: Develop data catalog and establish data protection policy. Create comprehensive inventories of data assets and formalize protective guidelines.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Operational Management. Stream: Data Protection. At maturity level 2, the organization maintains a comprehensive data catalog with formalized data protection policies governing the handling of different data classifications.

controls.title: Operational Management - Data Protection - Level 3
controls.description: Automate detection of policy non-compliance, and audit compliance periodically. Implement continuous monitoring systems and regularly review data catalog and protection policies.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Operational Management. Stream: Data Protection. At maturity level 3, data protection compliance is continuously monitored with automated detection of policy violations and regular audits of data catalog accuracy.

controls.title: Operational Management - System Decommissioning / Legacy Management - Level 1
controls.description: Decommission unused applications and services as identified. Manage customer upgrades and migrations individually.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Operational Management. Stream: System Decommissioning / Legacy Management. At maturity level 1, unused applications and services are decommissioned as identified, with individual management of customer migrations.

controls.title: Operational Management - System Decommissioning / Legacy Management - Level 2
controls.description: Develop repeatable decommissioning processes for unused systems and services, and for migration from legacy dependencies. Manage legacy migration roadmaps for customers.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Operational Management. Stream: System Decommissioning / Legacy Management. At maturity level 2, repeatable processes exist for system decommissioning and legacy migration, with defined roadmaps for customer transitions.

controls.title: Operational Management - System Decommissioning / Legacy Management - Level 3
controls.description: Proactively manage migration roadmaps, for both unsupported end-of-life dependencies, and legacy versions of delivered software.
controls.discussion: Business Function: Operations. Practice: Operational Management. Stream: System Decommissioning / Legacy Management. At maturity level 3, migration roadmaps are proactively managed for all end-of-life dependencies and legacy software versions, preventing unsupported systems from remaining in production.
